diffHtml:
Files changed (2) hide show
  1. src/virtual-review-discovery.mjs +142 -0
  2. src/virtual-review-store.mjs +848 -0
src/virtual-review-discovery.mjs ADDED
@@ -0,0 +1,142 @@
1
+ import fs from "node:fs/promises";
2
+ import path from "node:path";
3
+
4
+ import {
5
+ listVirtualReviews,
6
+ loadVirtualSource,
7
+ virtualReviewStateRoot,
8
+ } from "./virtual-review-store.mjs";
9
+
10
+ const shortSha = (sha) => typeof sha === "string" ? sha.slice(0, 8) : "";
11
+
12
+ const currentBoundaryFor = ({ baseSha, headSha, targetSha }) => ({
13
+ baseSha,
14
+ headSha,
15
+ targetSha,
16
+ });
17
+
18
+ const sourceBoundaryFor = (source) => ({
19
+ baseSha: source?.repository?.baseSha || "",
20
+ headSha: source?.repository?.headSha
21
+ || source?.endpoints?.to?.sha
22
+ || source?.branchCommit?.sha
23
+ || "",
24
+ targetSha: source?.repository?.targetSha || "",
25
+ });
26
+
27
+ const boundariesMatch = (source, current) => (
28
+ ["baseSha", "headSha", "targetSha"].every((name) => (
29
+ typeof source[name] === "string"
30
+ && source[name].length > 0
31
+ && source[name] === current[name]
32
+ ))
33
+ );
34
+
35
+ const canonicalInputRoot = async (value) => {
36
+ if (typeof value !== "string" || value.length === 0) {
37
+ throw new TypeError("Virtual review discovery requires a repository root");
38
+ }
39
+ return fs.realpath(path.resolve(value));
40
+ };
41
+
42
+ const canonicalStoredRoot = async (value, cache) => {
43
+ if (typeof value !== "string" || !path.isAbsolute(value)) return null;
44
+ const resolved = path.resolve(value);
45
+ if (!cache.has(resolved)) {
46
+ cache.set(resolved, fs.realpath(resolved).catch(() => null));
47
+ }
48
+ return cache.get(resolved);
49
+ };
50
+
51
+ const timestamp = (value) => {
52
+ const parsed = Date.parse(value);
53
+ return Number.isFinite(parsed) ? parsed : Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY;
54
+ };
55
+
56
+ const compareCandidates = (left, right) => {
57
+ if (left.state !== right.state) return left.state === "current" ? -1 : 1;
58
+ const dateDifference = timestamp(right.createdAt) - timestamp(left.createdAt);
59
+ if (dateDifference !== 0) return dateDifference;
60
+ if (left.revision !== right.revision) return right.revision - left.revision;
61
+ return left.reviewId.localeCompare(right.reviewId);
62
+ };
63
+
64
+ /**
65
+ * Find the best saved Virtual Review revision for one live Local MR identity.
66
+ *
67
+ * Repository, branch, and target-ref identity are intentionally stricter than
68
+ * freshness. A moved HEAD/base/target keeps the saved review discoverable but
69
+ * marks it stale; it never permits a review from another branch or target.
70
+ */
71
+ export const discoverVirtualReview = async ({
72
+ repoRoot,
73
+ repositoryRoot,
74
+ branchName,
75
+ targetRef,
76
+ baseSha,
77
+ headSha,
78
+ targetSha,
79
+ stateRoot = virtualReviewStateRoot(),
80
+ }) => {
81
+ if (typeof branchName !== "string" || branchName.length === 0) {
82
+ throw new TypeError("Virtual review discovery requires a branch name");
83
+ }
84
+ if (typeof targetRef !== "string" || targetRef.length === 0) {
85
+ throw new TypeError("Virtual review discovery requires a target ref");
86
+ }
87
+ const currentBoundary = currentBoundaryFor({ baseSha, headSha, targetSha });
88
+ if (Object.values(currentBoundary).some((sha) => typeof sha !== "string" || sha.length === 0)) {
89
+ throw new TypeError("Virtual review discovery requires current base, head, and target SHAs");
90
+ }
91
+
92
+ const canonicalRoot = await canonicalInputRoot(repoRoot ?? repositoryRoot);
93
+ const storedRootCache = new Map();
94
+ const candidates = [];
95
+ const reviews = await listVirtualReviews(stateRoot);
96
+
97
+ for (const review of reviews) {
98
+ if (typeof review?.reviewId !== "string" || !Array.isArray(review.revisions)) continue;
99
+ for (const revision of review.revisions) {
100
+ if (
101
+ !Number.isSafeInteger(revision?.revision)
102
+ || revision.revision < 1
103
+ || typeof revision.sourceId !== "string"
104
+ || typeof revision.createdAt !== "string"
105
+ || !Number.isFinite(Date.parse(revision.createdAt))
106
+ || typeof revision.title !== "string"
107
+ ) continue;
108
+ try {
109
+ const source = await loadVirtualSource(revision.sourceId, stateRoot);
110
+ const repository = source?.repository;
111
+ if (
112
+ !repository
113
+ || repository.branchName !== branchName
114
+ || repository.targetRef !== targetRef
115
+ || await canonicalStoredRoot(repository.root, storedRootCache) !== canonicalRoot
116
+ ) continue;
117
+
118
+ const sourceBoundary = sourceBoundaryFor(source);
119
+ const sourceSha = sourceBoundary.headSha;
120
+ candidates.push({
121
+ reviewId: review.reviewId,
122
+ revision: revision.revision,
123
+ sourceId: revision.sourceId,
124
+ state: boundariesMatch(sourceBoundary, currentBoundary) ? "current" : "stale",
125
+ sourceSha,
126
+ sourceShortSha: shortSha(sourceSha),
127
+ currentSha: headSha,
128
+ currentShortSha: shortSha(headSha),
129
+ sourceBoundary,
130
+ currentBoundary: { ...currentBoundary },
131
+ createdAt: revision.createdAt,
132
+ title: revision.title,
133
+ });
134
+ } catch {
135
+ // One missing or corrupt immutable source must not hide other reviews.
136
+ }
137
+ }
138
+ }
139
+
140
+ candidates.sort(compareCandidates);
141
+ return candidates[0] || null;
142
+ };
heading: Local MR: main → e524ea0 (initial commit)
title: Local MR: main → e524ea0 (initial commit)

focusedCommit.label: Human review index
focusedCommit.title: Review the MR in human order
focusedCommit.description: Deep review · docs-first, then dependency and risk order. Fourteen focused steps keep each reviewer question narrow without relying on source line numbers.
reviewFocus: V6 · Discover the right saved review

commits.subject: Read the contract before the implementation
commits.body: Start with the human-facing explanation of why Virtual Commits exist, how reviewers use them, and which invariants the implementation must preserve.
commits.description: Start with the human-facing explanation of why Virtual Commits exist, how reviewers use them, and which invariants the implementation must preserve.
commits.intent: Start with the human-facing explanation of why Virtual Commits exist, how reviewers use them, and which invariants the implementation must preserve.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Extract the source-freezing, block-conservation, and final-tree guarantees before reading their implementation.
Check that the concise README workflow agrees with the detailed guide.

risk.reason: The documentation defines the reviewer's mental model and must not promise guarantees the code does not enforce.

commits.subject: Map the CLI and agent contract
commits.body: Follow the user and agent entry points from source capture through manifest submission and structured validation.
commits.description: Follow the user and agent entry points from source capture through manifest submission and structured validation.
commits.intent: Follow the user and agent entry points from source capture through manifest submission and structured validation.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Trace manifest input from the CLI boundary through schema and block-assignment validation.
Confirm the bundled Skill describes the same protocol and safety boundary enforced by the CLI.

risk.reason: This contract decides which frozen source an agent may reorganize and which invalid plans are rejected.

commits.subject: Freeze the source and define stable change blocks
commits.body: Verify merge-base selection and the deterministic block model before any change is reordered into a Virtual Commit.
commits.description: Verify merge-base selection and the deterministic block model before any change is reordered into a Virtual Commit.
commits.intent: Verify merge-base selection and the deterministic block model before any change is reordered into a Virtual Commit.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Check that target-only history cannot leak into the frozen review range.
Verify stable identities for text, binary, rename, delete, and submodule changes.

risk.reason: Every later conservation guarantee depends on freezing the right Diff and naming each block exactly once.

commits.subject: Reconstruct the exact target tree
commits.body: Follow cumulative block application from the frozen base through every Virtual state to the real feature commit tree.
commits.description: Follow cumulative block application from the frozen base through every Virtual state to the real feature commit tree.
commits.intent: Follow cumulative block application from the frozen base through every Virtual state to the real feature commit tree.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Verify missing, duplicate, unknown, or malformed block assignments fail closed.
Confirm final-tree equality is checked after materialization rather than inferred from block counts.

risk.reason: A flaw here could silently omit or alter code while presenting a plausible review route.

commits.subject: Protect immutable review history
commits.body: Audit revision storage, locking, atomic writes, permissions, progress, and cleanup.
commits.description: Audit revision storage, locking, atomic writes, permissions, progress, and cleanup.
commits.intent: Audit revision storage, locking, atomic writes, permissions, progress, and cleanup.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Check concurrent revision creation and recovery from interrupted writes.
Confirm permissions and cleanup match a private-source-code storage boundary.

risk.reason: Mutable or weakly protected snapshots would undermine reproducibility and source confidentiality.

commits.subject: Discover the right saved review
commits.body: Verify that the default command attaches the newest compatible revision to the current repository comparison.
commits.description: Verify that the default command attaches the newest compatible revision to the current repository comparison.
commits.intent: Verify that the default command attaches the newest compatible revision to the current repository comparison.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Check repository, target, base, and head matching before a saved revision is attached.
Confirm stale revisions remain visible without being mistaken for the current source.

risk.reason: Choosing the wrong saved review can put valid Virtual Commit labels over unrelated source code.

commits.subject: Pin runtime identity and source handoff
commits.body: Follow a stored revision into its runtime while keeping repository, source, and revision identity immutable.
commits.description: Follow a stored revision into its runtime while keeping repository, source, and revision identity immutable.
commits.intent: Follow a stored revision into its runtime while keeping repository, source, and revision identity immutable.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Confirm opening a stored review cannot silently fall back to live HEAD or another repository.
Check stale-source reporting without mutating the frozen source.

risk.reason: An identity mix-up could show valid code from the wrong source while retaining a trusted review URL.

commits.subject: Serve Virtual comparisons and context
commits.body: Audit selection parsing, tree-to-tree comparison, context expansion, previews, and Real or Virtual navigation.
commits.description: Audit selection parsing, tree-to-tree comparison, context expansion, previews, and Real or Virtual navigation.
commits.intent: Audit selection parsing, tree-to-tree comparison, context expansion, previews, and Real or Virtual navigation.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Verify every selection resolves to the intended before and after Virtual states.
Check that context, previews, IDs, paths, and revisions remain scoped to the frozen source.

risk.reason: This boundary turns stored state into reviewer-visible code and handles untrusted URL input.

commits.subject: Integrate Virtual history into the shared server
commits.body: Verify discovery, source attachment, routes, and version metadata remain consistent in the normal Local MR server.
commits.description: Verify discovery, source attachment, routes, and version metadata remain consistent in the normal Local MR server.
commits.intent: Verify discovery, source attachment, routes, and version metadata remain consistent in the normal Local MR server.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Trace how the default Local MR page discovers and attaches a compatible Virtual review.
Check route and cache identity when switching between Real and Virtual sources.

risk.reason: A bad handoff can make the normal review page display metadata or Diff content from another comparison.

commits.subject: Navigate the shared review workspace
commits.body: Read the browser state machine for modes, ranges, revisions, Real and Virtual switching, context, previews, and restored selections.
commits.description: Read the browser state machine for modes, ranges, revisions, Real and Virtual switching, context, previews, and restored selections.
commits.intent: Read the browser state machine for modes, ranges, revisions, Real and Virtual switching, context, previews, and restored selections.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Follow a selection through URL state, data loading, source switching, and history restoration.
Check that syntax, context, previews, and controls never imply a different comparison from the Diff on screen.

risk.reason: A misleading workspace can make reviewers approve the wrong comparison even when server data is correct.

commits.subject: Prove the core invariants with adversarial tests
commits.body: Match source freezing, block conservation, manifest validation, and final-tree equality to executable failure cases.
commits.description: Match source freezing, block conservation, manifest validation, and final-tree equality to executable failure cases.
commits.intent: Match source freezing, block conservation, manifest validation, and final-tree equality to executable failure cases.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Look for target merges, drift, missing blocks, duplicate blocks, binary data, renames, and submodules.
Confirm negative tests prove fail-closed behavior rather than only friendly error text.

risk.reason: These tests are the executable evidence that reordering changes presentation rather than code.

commits.subject: Stress persistence, discovery, and installation
commits.body: Inspect regressions for concurrent revisions, saved-review matching, frozen runtime identity, attachment, and package installation.
commits.description: Inspect regressions for concurrent revisions, saved-review matching, frozen runtime identity, attachment, and package installation.
commits.intent: Inspect regressions for concurrent revisions, saved-review matching, frozen runtime identity, attachment, and package installation.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Check concurrent writers, immutable revisions, stale repositories, and compatible review discovery.
Verify the installed package includes the CLI and bundled Skill needed by the documented workflow.

risk.reason: Cross-process and installed-package failures often escape core unit tests.

commits.subject: Exercise the complete review experience
commits.body: Finish authored behavior with the remaining integration and browser regressions for rendering, context, caching, previews, and read state.
commits.description: Finish authored behavior with the remaining integration and browser regressions for rendering, context, caching, previews, and read state.
commits.intent: Finish authored behavior with the remaining integration and browser regressions for rendering, context, caching, previews, and read state.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Confirm browser tests operate controls and inspect the resulting Diff rather than only static markup.
Check cache, context, Markdown, read-state, and renderer regressions around the shared workspace.

risk.reason: This is where otherwise-correct subsystems combine into the comparison a human actually approves.

commits.subject: Finish with release metadata
commits.body: Review package wiring and change notes after the implementation and its evidence are understood.
commits.description: Review package wiring and change notes after the implementation and its evidence are understood.
commits.intent: Review package wiring and change notes after the implementation and its evidence are understood.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Check that package entry points and scripts expose the documented workflow.
Confirm both change logs describe the same shipped capability without overstating it.

risk.reason: These files do not implement the feature, but stale packaging or release notes can make the shipped interface diverge from the reviewed code.

branchCommit.subject: feat(virtual-commits): let humans review AI-sized diffs in reading order
branchCommit.author: Ne9roni

branchCommit.subject: feat(virtual-commits): let humans review AI-sized diffs in reading order

demo.hint: Deep review · V1 docs → V14 release metadata · complete range = Real
